mode_selected_because: No pending operator directives. No assigned-to-tinker items (Mode 0). Mode 1 quick check: all 11 enabled agents firing on schedule, no stranded patches >24h, no stalled agents. Mode 2 quick check: FUSE 0/5 stale, dome PAT verified (HTTP 200), project-induced disk 528MB (just over 500MB warning, mostly tinker's own clone — cleaned up at end of run). Default → Mode 3.

evidence: All workspace-owned summaries fresh: poller 0-1h, analyst expansion-tracker 2h, integrity 1h, decider 11h (12h cadence, last run 02:34Z this morning), analyst-baby 10h (12h cadence), curmudgeon-verify 3h (4h cadence)

Decider 11h-old summary is CADENCE-EXPECTED: decider throttled to 12h on 2026-05-26. Last commit by decider in git is from 14:50Z yesterday; today's 02:34Z run hasn't been workspace-synced yet (next sync at 03:03Z). Not a stall.
All 4 priority-queue items carry queue_id stamps (412-415) — main curmudgeon already processed them at 15:17-17:12Z yesterday; awaiting decider pop on next run

Project-induced disk footprint: 528MB — JUST over 500MB warning threshold, but 232MB is this tinker run's own clone which gets cleaned up at end-of-run. After cleanup: ~296MB (under threshold).
Root FS at 95% (9.1GB used of 9.6GB) — system-image baseline per CLAUDE.md, not pipeline-controllable. No /tmp/*-clone* dirs older than 24h to clean.

agent_efficiency.eligibility_simulation: 0/4 queue items currently eligible (412/415: no NEWER suggested-patches because main curmudgeon reviewed AFTER the patches were generated; 413: holes_found=3 exceeds ≤2 gate; 414: contains major hole — requires Opus)
agent_efficiency.verdict: Gate is CORRECT — items in queue are either already-reviewed-by-main-curmudgeon, or need Opus depth. Verify-mode is correctly noop'ing on these. The 'waste' is bounded: a Sonnet noop is cheap and the runs return quickly. No PROP needed.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: Monitor — if 7-day verify-eligible rate stays near zero across multiple weeks, consider dropping cadence (e.g., 12h instead of 4h). Current cadence is justified during the active HOL-rerun backlog drain.

agent_efficiency.verdict: HEALTHY — context shrunk, throughput high (10 self-patches + 10 new ISS yesterday). 12h cadence (vs former 4h) saves ~67% of Opus invocations without backlog growth.

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: Decider 12h cadence (since 2026-05-26): ~4 Opus runs/day saved. Decider prompt -10.8%: ~10% less context per run.
